summary: Complete backend testing of SCI Lease parser fix. All 11 tests passed verifying the 2-row offset bug has been fixed. The fix correctly uses zip() to align vehicle names with rate rows instead of using the same index for both tables.
critical_code_review_comments: The parse_sci_lease() function fix in pdfplumber_parser.py correctly builds separate filtered lists for vehicle_names and rate_rows, then zips them together
Names table (col 1) has different header rows than rates table - the old code used same index causing 2-row offset
March 2026 data correctly shows Grand Caravan SXT with alt_rates starting at 4.99% (not 3.49% old bug)
All expected Lease Cash values verified: Compass North=$3,500, Ram Sport Rebel=$8,250, Durango Hellcat=$15,500, Ram Laramie=$11,500
context_for_next_testing_agent: SCI Lease parser fix fully verified. Backend tests in test_sci_lease_fix.py cover all requirements from the problem statement.

POST_extract_pdf_March2026.result: 93 retail programs + 74 SCI lease vehicles extracted

Grand_Caravan_SXT_alt_rates.result: Alt rates start at 4.99% (not 3.49% old bug)

all_2026_vehicles_have_rates.result: All 33 vehicles have either standard_rates or alternative_rates

parse_sci_lease_direct_test.result: Direct parser function test verified alignment

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The SCI Lease PDF has two tables per page - a names table (col 1 = vehicle names) and a rates table (30+ cols with rate data). These tables have different numbers of header rows: names table had vehicle names starting at row 14, but rates table had rate data starting at row 12. The old code used the same index `ri` for both tables, causing a 2-row offset where each vehicle got rates from 2 positions ahead.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: The fix builds separate filtered lists: vehicle_names (from names_t) and rate_rows (from rates_t), then uses zip() to pair them together correctly instead of accessing both tables with the same row index.
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All test cases pass confirming the fix: vehicle counts match expected values, Lease Cash amounts are correctly mapped to the right vehicles, and alternative rates (like Grand Caravan SXT 4.99%) are aligned properly.
